title: papaver horridulum
intro: Papaver horridulum, also known as the Wyoming poppy or California poppy, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been employed to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation due to its sedative and calming effects, as well as its potential to alleviate pain and discomfort. The herbal preparations of Papaver horridulum include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
